zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Barne of Woolwich於1568年5月11日出生在英國肯特武爾維其時，他的父親George Barne43歲，母親Anne Garrard39歲。他於1586年5月11日與Anne Sandys在英國諾廷加姆爾素維爾結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他在英國肯特武爾維其被列為justice of the peace。他去世於1619年5月7日在英國英格蘭米德萊可特查阿培爾享年50歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可特查阿培爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Barne of Woolwich于1568年5月11日出生在英国肯特武爾維其时，他的父亲George Barne43岁，母亲Anne Garrard39岁。他于1586年5月11日与Anne Sandys在英国諾廷加姆爾素維爾结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他在英国肯特武爾維其被列为justice of the peace。他去世于1619年5月7日在英国英格兰米德萊可特查阿培爾享年50岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可特查阿培爾。

nameMeanings.name: Barne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Barn
meanings: Scottish:  see  Barne   and  compare  Berne  .

variants.name: Barner
meanings: English: in southern England a habitational name for someone who lived by a barn, from Middle English berner ‘dweller by the barn’. Elsewhere possibly a variant of Berner .
Scottish: unexplained.
North German: derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Barnher or Bernher (see Berner 6).

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: English: locative name from either of two places in Lancs, namely Bispham near Blackpool ( Bischopham, 1086 in Domesday Book ) or Bispham in Croston.

variants.name: Berne
meanings: French: from a short form of the personal name Bernard or any other name containing the ancient Germanic element ber(n) ‘bear’.
Altered form of Swiss German   Berni  .
English and Scottish: variant of Barne (see Barnes 2) or Burn .

variants.name: Bearne
meanings: Scots: Bearn, a child, a human creature

variants.name: Byrne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Aidan, Liam, Niall, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, John Patrick, Keelin, Kieran, Siobhan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Broin ‘descendant of Bran’, a personal name based on bran ‘raven’. Bran was the name of a son of the King of Leinster, who died at Cologne in 1052, and also of the hero of an 8th century voyage tale.
Irish: in some cases, possibly from Ó Birn ‘descendant of Biorn’, Irish form of the Old Norse personal name Bjǫrn.

variants.name: Child
meanings: English:

nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Barne of Woolwich was born on 11 May 1568, in Woolwich, Kent, England, his father, George Barne, was 43 and his mother, Anne Garrard, was 39. He married Anne Sandys on 11 May 1586, in Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as justice of the peace in Woolwich, Kent, England. He died on 7 May 1619, in Whitechapel, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50, and was buried in Whitechapel, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Barne of Woolwich est né le 11 mai 1568, à Woolwich, Kent, Angleterre, son père, George Barne, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Anne Garrard, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Anne Sandys le 11 mai 1586, à Southwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est justice of the peace à Woolwich, Kent, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 7 mai 1619, à Whitechapel, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Whitechapel, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Barne of Woolwich am 11. Mai 1568 in Woolwich, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater George Barne 43 und seine Mutter Anne Garrard 39. Er hat am 11. Mai 1586 in Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England Anne Sandys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 11 Söhne und 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als justice of the peace in Woolwich, Kent, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 7. Mai 1619 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Whitechapel, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Whitechapel, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Barne of Woolwich è nato il il 11 maggio 1568 nel Woolwich, Kent, England, il suo padre, George Barne, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Anne Garrard, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Anne Sandys il 11 maggio 1586, nel Southwell, Nottinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 11 figli e 1 figlia. La sua occupazione è indicata come justice of the peace nel Woolwich, Kent, England. Morì il 7 maggio 1619, nel Whitechapel, Middlesex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Whitechapel, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Barne of Woolwichが1568年5月11日にイギリスケントウルウィチで生まれた時，父George Barneは43歳，母Anne Garrardは39歳であった。1586年5月11日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアソウスウエルでAnne Sandysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。イギリスケントウルウィチでの職業はjustice of the peaceと記録されている。彼は1619年5月7日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウィテチャペルで50歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスウィテチャペルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Barne of Woolwich(이)가 1568년 5월 11일 영국 켄트 보위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Barne(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Anne Garrard(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1586년 5월 11일 영국 노틴그함실르 소우스웰에서 Anne Sandys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 켄트 보위치에서 justice of the peace이었다. 그는 1619년 5월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 휘테차플에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 휘테차플에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Barne of Woolwich nasceu em 11 de maio de 1568, em Woolwich, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, George Barne, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Anne Garrard, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Sandys em 11 de maio de 1586, em Southwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 11 filhos e 1 filha. Sua profissão aparece como justice of the peace em Woolwich, Kent, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 7 de maio de 1619, em Whitechapel, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Whitechapel, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Barne of Woolwich родился 11 мая 1568 г. в местности вулвич, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени George Barne было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Anne Garrard, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Sandys 11 мая 1586 г. в местности соутвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии justice of the peace в местности вулвич, кэнт, Англия. Он умер 7 мая 1619 г. в местности уитечапел, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности уитечапел, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.London St Mary Aldermanbury, Middlesex,: London St Mary Aldermanbury, Middlesex, Inglaterra
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Barne of Woolwich nació el 11 de mayo de 1568, en Woolwich, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, George Barne, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Anne Garrard, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Anne Sandys el 11 de mayo de 1586, en Southwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 1 hija. Su ocupación registrada era justice of the peace en Woolwich, Kent, Inglaterra. Murió el 7 de mayo de 1619, en Whitechapel, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Whitechapel, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
